title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Jimmy Sycamore, Lois, and Dr. Carruthers depart, Inspector Bartlett pulls Eileen Bündel into a private living room. Bündel recounts her experiences at the Seven Dials Club and asserts that the woman she saw there, Anna, was indeed Countess Razkai, whom she identified by a specific mole. Bündel argues that the Countess's behavior—specifically her use of a flashlight to search for a book—was suspicious and suggests that her fainting during the struggle was feigned. While acknowledging the Countess's suspicious conduct, the Inspector insists on caution to avoid diplomatic complications with the embassy. He explains his strategy of targeting larger threats rather than "small fry" and asks Bündel not to raise further suspicions, noting that he must report to Sir Stanley Digby.

The following morning in the library, Inspector Bartlett meets with George Lomax, Sir Osbert Cutt, and Jimmy Sycamore to review the evidence. The Inspector presents several labeled items, including a Leopold automatic pistol and two bullets: a .45 caliber bullet from Mr. Sceges's Colt that hit a cedar tree, and a .25 caliber Mauser bullet that lodged in an armchair. He notes that the shooter wore gloves, leaving no fingerprints. Regarding a gun found near the terrace, the Inspector observes that the only footprints on the lawn belong to Sir Osbert and Miss Wade. Based on the indentation in the grass, the Inspector and George conclude the gun was thrown from the terrace rather than dropped by a fleeing man. The meeting is interrupted by Mrs. Cutt, who arrives to bring medicine to Sir Osbert.
